title: Real Time PCR of Mouse Liver Tissue
author: Mei Zhao
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ri7d4hn
materials: Assay has been used on a LightCycler 480 instrument (Roche Diagnostics) using 96-place rotor discs.Total reaction volume is 20μL.Prepare sufficient for number of reaction plus a 'dead volume' usually 2 extra. Adjust as necessary if using a robotic dispenser.
NTCs should not produce a curve

steps.title: The definition used for a satisfactory...
steps.instruction: The definition used for a satisfactory positive result from a realtime fluorogenic PCR should include each of the following:

steps.title: A sigmoidal curve – the trace...
steps.instruction: A sigmoidal curve – the trace travels horizontally, curves upward, continues in an exponential rise and followed by a curve towards a horizontal plateau phase

steps.title: A suitable level of fluorescence intensity...
steps.instruction: A suitable level of fluorescence intensity as measured in comparison to a positive control (y-axis)

steps.title: A defined threshold (CT) value which...
steps.instruction: A defined threshold (CT) value which the fluorescent curve has clearly exceeded (Fig.1 arrow) and which sits early in the log-linear phase and is <40 cycles

steps.title: A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or...
steps.instruction: A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or a curve that crosses the threshold with a CT value >40 cycles is considered a negative result
